notes: All 3 steps completed. Extended invalid bounds tests with 5 new -1 cases and valid bounds tests with 4 new value=1 cases. All 74 config tests pass. No config.py changes needed — validation already existed.

summary: Add has_transcript(media_id), has_detections(media_id), has_boundaries(media_id), has_faces(media_id), has_embeddings(media_id), has_audio_events(media_id) convenience methods to CatalogDB. These return bool by checking row existence in the respective tables via SELECT 1 ... LIMIT 1.; Write failing tests for _run_ingest resume: test_ingest_skips_already_ingested_media (when db.get_media returns non-None for a media_id, skip insert+normalize), test_ingest_logs_resume_counts (logs 'Resuming INGEST: N/M files already ingested'), test_ingest_force_reprocesses_all (when force=True, reprocess even existing media); Implement resume logic in _run_ingest: before insert_media, check db.get_media(media_id) and skip if already exists. Add force kwarg (default False). Log resume counts.; Write failing tests for _run_analyze per-pass resume: test_analyze_skips_transcribed_media (when db.has_transcript returns True, skip asr.transcribe_media for that media), test_analyze_skips_detected_media (when db.has_detections returns True, skip objects.detect_objects), and similar for boundaries/faces/embeddings/audio_events. test_analyze_logs_resume_counts per pass. test_analyze_force_reprocesses_all.; Implement per-pass resume in _run_analyze: before each analysis call, check the corresponding has_* method. Add force kwarg (default False). Log per-pass resume counts (e.g. 'Resuming ANALYZE: 47/100 media already transcribed, processing remaining 53').; Write failing tests for _run_classify resume: test_classify_skips_when_clusters_exist (when db.get_activity_clusters returns non-empty and all have labels, skip). test_classify_force_clears_and_reprocesses.; Implement resume logic in _run_classify: check if activity clusters already exist and are labeled, skip if so. Add force kwarg (default False).

Implement resume logic in _run_upload (check db.get_upload before uploading, skip if exists, force kwarg). Add force parameter to PipelineOrchestrator.__init__ and propagate to stage.func calls. Add --force flag to CLI _common_options decorator and wire through to PipelineOrchestrator and individual stage calls. All 20 steps + 1 prerequisite complete. 227 tests pass (91 db + 100 orchestrator + 36 cli).

Write test for SSE notification listener try/catch error handling (static JS source analysis). Fix app.js to wrap JSON.parse in try/catch with console.error logging. All 17 web app tests pass. All 14 steps + 2 prerequisites complete — plan fully done.

All 12 steps complete for Task 37 (run tracking + event emission). Added: (1) pipeline_runs record creation at run() start with uuid4 run_id, ISO started_at, status='running', config_snapshot, budget_remaining_seconds; (2) _emit_event helper calling db.insert_event with JSON payload serialization and DEBUG logging; (3) stage_started/stage_completed/stage_error events at each stage transition with current_stage updates on the run; (4) run finalization with status='completed'|'failed', finished_at, wall_clock_seconds; (5) budget_remaining_seconds tracking after each stage when budget is set; (6) integration tests with real in-memory CatalogDB. 141 tests pass (20 new + 121 existing).

Write TestRunTrackingInit class with tests: (1) test_run_creates_pipeline_run_record — after run() with mock stages, db.insert_run called once with 32-char hex run_id, started_at ISO string, status='running', config_snapshot is a string; (2) test_run_stores_run_id_on_self — after run(), orch._run_id is a 32-char hex string; (3) test_run_passes_budget_to_insert_run — when budget_seconds=3600, insert_run called with budget_remaining_seconds=3600; Add imports (uuid4, datetime, timezone) to orchestrator.py. At start of run(), generate run_id=uuid4().hex, started_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(), store self._run_id=run_id and self._db=db, call db.insert_run(run_id, started_at=started_at, config_snapshot=str(config), status='running', budget_remaining_seconds=self.budget_seconds); Write TestEmitEvent class with tests: (1) test_emit_event_calls_insert_event — set orch._db to a MagicMock, call _emit_event('test_event', stage='INGEST'), verify db.insert_event called with event_type='test_event', stage='INGEST'; (2) test_emit_event_serializes_payload_to_json — call with payload={'key': 'val'}, verify payload_json='{"key": "val"}'; (3) test_emit_event_none_payload — call without payload, verify payload_json=None; (4) test_emit_event_logs_at_debug_level — verify debug log emitted; Add _emit_event(self, event_type, stage=None, job_id=None, payload=None) method to PipelineOrchestrator. Calls self._db.insert_event(event_type, stage=stage, job_id=job_id, payload_json=json.dumps(payload) if payload is not None else None). Logs at debug level.; Write TestStageTransitionEvents class with tests: (1) test_stage_started_event_emitted_for_each_stage — run with all mock stages, verify db.insert_event called with event_type='stage_started' and correct stage name for each of the 9 stages; (2) test_stage_completed_event_emitted_with_elapsed — verify 'stage_completed' events include payload with 'elapsed' key; (3) test_stage_error_event_emitted — make one stage raise, verify 'stage_error' event with error message in payload; (4) test_current_stage_updated_via_update_run — verify db.update_run called with current_stage=name before each stage runs; In run() stage loop, before calling stage.func: emit 'stage_started' event and call db.update_run(run_id, current_stage=stage_name). After successful stage: emit 'stage_completed' event with payload={'elapsed': elapsed}. In exception handler: emit 'stage_error' event with payload={'error': str(exc)}.; Write TestRunFinalization class with tests: (1) test_run_completed_status_on_success — when all stages pass, db.update_run called with status='completed', finished_at (ISO string), wall_clock_seconds (float >= 0); (2) test_run_failed_status_on_error — when a stage errors, final update_run has status='failed'; (3) test_run_completed_event_emitted — 'run_completed' event with payload={'duration': float}; (4) test_run_failed_event_emitted — 'run_failed' event emitted when pipeline has errors; After the stage loop and summary logging in run(), determine status='failed' if errored_stages else 'completed'. Call db.update_run(run_id, finished_at=datetime.now(UTC).isoformat(), status=status, wall_clock_seconds=total_elapsed). Emit 'run_completed' or 'run_failed' event with payload={'duration': total_elapsed}.; Write TestBudgetRemainingTracking class with tests: (1) test_budget_remaining_written_after_each_stage — with budget_seconds=3600, verify db.update_run called with budget_remaining_seconds after each stage; (2) test_no_budget_remaining_when_no_budget — with budget_seconds=None, verify update_run not called with budget_remaining_seconds for per-stage updates (only called for current_stage and finalization); In the progress reporting section of run() (after each stage), if budget_seconds is not None: compute remaining = budget_seconds - cumulative, call db.update_run(run_id, budget_remaining_seconds=remaining).; Write TestRunTrackingIntegration class using real in-memory CatalogDB (catalog_db fixture): (1) test_full_run_creates_db_records — run orchestrator with mock stage funcs and real CatalogDB, verify pipeline_runs row exists with correct status='completed' and wall_clock_seconds > 0; (2) test_full_run_creates_events — verify pipeline_events table has stage_started + stage_completed events for each stage plus run_completed; (3) test_dry_run_still_creates_run_record — dry_run=True still creates pipeline_runs record; Run full test suite (pytest tests/test_orchestrator.py -x) to verify all existing + new tests pass. Fix any issues.
Debug fix for: Failures: lint issues, type errors

Completed all 6 resilience steps (13-18) for Task 37. Added: (1) _emit_event swallows exceptions with WARNING log, (2) db.insert_run failure sets _run_id=None sentinel causing graceful degradation — pipeline runs without tracking, (3) each db.update_run call individually wrapped in try/except. 152 tests pass (11 new resilience tests + 141 existing). All 18 steps now complete.

Add 'sse-starlette' to pyproject.toml in the Web dependencies section alongside fastapi/uvicorn/jinja2; Write tests/test_sse.py with fixtures: (1) sse_db fixture that creates an in-memory CatalogDB with autocommit, (2) sse_app fixture that calls create_app with a tmp_path db but then overrides app.state.db_path to point to the in-memory db file or patches the dependency, (3) sse_client fixture with Starlette TestClient. Write TestSSEEndpointBasic class with test_sse_endpoint_returns_200_event_stream — GET /api/events returns 200 with content-type text/event-stream.; Create autopilot/web/routes/sse.py with: (1) APIRouter with prefix '', (2) get_db dependency that creates CatalogDB from request.app.state.db_path, (3) GET /api/events endpoint that returns EventSourceResponse from a minimal async generator yielding nothing (just awaits forever with asyncio.sleep). Update autopilot/web/routes/__init__.py to export the router. Register SSE router in app.py via app.include_router(sse_router). Make the basic 200 + content-type test pass.; Write TestSSEEventDelivery class: (1) test_events_delivered_in_order — insert 3 events via CatalogDB (stage_started, job_started, stage_completed), connect to SSE endpoint, verify all 3 events arrive in correct order; (2) test_event_has_correct_sse_fields — verify each SSE event has id field (event_id), event field (event_type), and data field (JSON with event_type, stage, job_id); (3) test_event_data_payload_structure — verify the JSON data payload contains event_type, stage, job_id, and original payload_json data.; Implement the full async event generator in sse.py: poll db.get_events_since(last_event_id) every 1 second using asyncio.sleep(1). For each event, yield a ServerSentEvent with id=str(event_id), event=event_type, data=json.dumps({event_type, stage, job_id, ...payload_json parsed}). Track last_event_id across polls. Make event delivery tests pass.; Write TestSSEReconnection class: (1) test_last_event_id_header_resumes_from_position — insert 5 events, connect with Last-Event-ID: 3 header, verify only events 4 and 5 are received; (2) test_missing_last_event_id_starts_from_zero — connect without header, verify all events are received; (3) test_invalid_last_event_id_starts_from_zero — connect with Last-Event-ID: 'abc' (non-numeric), verify all events are received.; Implement Last-Event-ID support: read request.headers.get('last-event-id', '0'), parse as int with fallback to 0, use as initial last_event_id for get_events_since(). Make reconnection tests pass.; Write TestSSEPruning class: (1) test_old_events_pruned_after_polls — insert a backdated event (>24h old via raw SQL) and a recent event, trigger SSE endpoint for enough polls, verify the old event was deleted from the DB; (2) test_recent_events_not_pruned — insert only recent events, after polling, verify all events still exist in DB.

All 12 steps + 1 prerequisite complete for Task 43 (media list page). Added: (1) has_captions() method to CatalogDB following existing has_* pattern, (2) query_media() method with pagination, text/status/date filters, sort whitelist, and EXISTS-subquery analysis flags, (3) GET /api/media JSON endpoint with all query params, (4) GET /media HTML page with media/list.html extending base.html, (5) partials/media_row.html with analysis badges (ASR/YOLO/Face/Emb/Audio/Cap), (6) HTMX partial support (HX-Request header returns HTML rows), (7) duration formatting, resolution display, empty state, pagination navigation. 30 tests pass (3 has_captions + 8 query_media + 6 API JSON + 5 HTML page + 4 HTMX partial + 4 integration). 117 total tests pass with no regressions.

Create test file tests/test_media_detail.py with shared fixtures: catalog_db (from conftest), media_db_path/media_db (file-backed DB), detail_seeded_db (media file 'test1' with full metadata including codec='h264', fps=30.0, resolution 1920x1080, gps, sha256_prefix, audio_channels=2, metadata_json with extra fields, plus: transcript with segments_json containing 3 segments with timestamps/speakers, detections across 3 frames with objects like 'person'/'car'/'dog', 2 faces on separate frames assigned to cluster_id=1, face_cluster with label='Alice', 2 audio events with events_json containing classified events, 3 clip_embeddings, and a second media 'test2' with no analysis data), detail_app, detail_client.; Write failing tests for CatalogDB.get_media_detail(media_id) in tests/test_media_detail.py: (1) returns None for non-existent media_id; (2) returns dict with 'media' key containing all media_files columns for existing media; (3) includes 'transcript' key with parsed segments list and language; (4) includes 'detections' key with list of detection rows; (5) includes 'faces' key with list of face rows; (6) includes 'audio_events' key with list of audio event rows; (7) includes 'embeddings' key with count of embeddings; (8) returns empty lists/None for media with no analysis data (test2).; Implement CatalogDB.get_media_detail(media_id) in autopilot/db.py. Returns None if media_id not found. Otherwise returns dict with keys: 'media' (from get_media), 'transcript' (from get_transcript, or None), 'detections' (from get_detections_for_media), 'faces' (from get_faces_for_media), 'audio_events' (from get_audio_events_for_media), 'embeddings' (from get_embeddings_for_media), 'embedding_count' (len of embeddings list), 'face_clusters' (dict mapping cluster_id to cluster info).; Write failing tests for GET /api/media/{media_id} JSON endpoint: (1) returns 404 for non-existent media_id; (2) returns 200 with JSON containing 'media', 'transcript', 'detections', 'faces', 'audio_events', 'embeddings' keys for existing media; (3) media section has all expected fields (codec, fps, resolution, etc.).; Implement GET /api/media/{media_id} route in media.py. Uses _get_db(request), calls db.get_media_detail(media_id), returns 404 HTTPException if None, otherwise returns the detail dict as JSON.; Write failing tests for GET /api/media/{media_id}/transcript: (1) returns 404 for missing media; (2) returns 200 with parsed segments list and language for media with transcript; (3) each segment has 'start', 'end', 'text', 'speaker' fields. And GET /api/media/{media_id}/detections: (1) returns 404 for missing media; (2) returns 200 with detection summary including 'total_detections' count, 'classes' dict with per-class counts, 'frame_count' (number of frames with detections).; Implement GET /api/media/{media_id}/transcript and GET /api/media/{media_id}/detections routes. Transcript endpoint parses segments_json into list of segment dicts. Detections endpoint aggregates across all frames: parses each frame's detections_json, counts total detections, builds per-class counts, and reports frame count.; Write failing tests for GET /media/{media_id} HTML detail page: (1) returns 404 for non-existent media_id; (2) returns 200 with text/html for existing media; (3) contains tab navigation with labels 'Metadata', 'Transcript', 'Detections', 'Faces', 'Audio Events', 'Embeddings'; (4) extends base.html (contains 'Autopilot Video' nav); (5) metadata tab content rendered by default (shows codec, resolution, duration); (6) tabs have hx-get attributes pointing to /media/{id}/tab/{name}.; Implement GET /media/{media_id} route and create templates/media/detail.html extending base.html. Tab navigation bar with HTMX hx-get attributes. Default active tab is metadata. Create partials/tab_metadata.html showing: codec, FPS, resolution (WxH), audio channels, duration (formatted), file path, SHA-256 prefix, dedup status, GPS coordinates, created_at timestamp, and parsed metadata_json fields.; Write failing tests for HTMX tab endpoints GET /media/{media_id}/tab/{tab_name}: (1) transcript tab returns HTML with timestamped lines like '[00:00:10]' and speaker labels; (2) transcript tab shows language indicator; (3) detections tab returns HTML with object class names and counts; (4) detections tab shows total detection count and frame coverage.; Implement HTMX tab endpoint GET /media/{media_id}/tab/{tab_name} in media.py that dispatches to tab-specific rendering logic. Create partials/tab_transcript.html (scrollable, time-stamped '[HH:MM:SS] Speaker: text' lines with language indicator) and partials/tab_detections.html (summary with top classes, total count, frame coverage, expandable grouped list by object class).; Write failing tests for remaining HTMX tab partials: (1) faces tab shows cluster grouping with cluster label 'Alice' and face count; (2) audio events tab shows timeline entries with event class names; (3) embeddings tab shows 'X of Y frames sampled (Z%)' format.; Create partials/tab_faces.html (grid grouped by cluster_id, showing cluster label from face_clusters table, count of appearances per cluster), partials/tab_audio_events.html (timeline-style list with parsed events_json, dominant event per time window), and partials/tab_embeddings.html (embedding count vs estimated total frames as 'X of Y frames sampled (Z%)').

Completed remaining 4 steps (13-16) for Task 44 (media detail page). Step 13: verified failing test for media_row.html filename link (committed in prior iteration as d9d5a43). Step 14: wrapped filename in <a href='/media/{id}'> with hover styling. Step 15: wrote 7 integration tests covering all detail endpoints, tabs, JSON APIs, empty-analysis graceful handling, and 404s. Step 16: verified edge cases (zero fps, null metadata, orphan face clusters, empty detections) all render gracefully — no code changes needed. All 40 media detail tests pass + 187 related tests pass with no regressions. All 16 steps + 1 prerequisite now complete.

Ensure all tests pass end-to-end. Verify templates render gracefully with missing data.

Create tests/test_dashboard.py with fixtures: file-backed CatalogDB seeded with a running pipeline run (run_id='run-1', started_at, current_stage='analyze', budget_remaining_seconds=3600), jobs across multiple stages (e.g. 5 done + 2 running in ingest, 1 running + 2 pending in analyze, 1 error in classify), initialized default gates with some modified (analyze=manual), and a few error events. Fixtures: dashboard_db_path, dashboard_db, dashboard_seeded_db, dashboard_app, dashboard_client. Follows patterns from test_sse.py and test_media_detail.py.; Write failing tests for GET /dashboard: (1) returns 200 with text/html; (2) contains all 9 stage names (ingest, analyze, classify, narrate, script, edl, source, render, upload); (3) extends base.html (contains 'Autopilot Video' nav); (4) contains 'Dashboard' in page title; (5) when no active run exists, shows 'Pipeline not running' banner.; Create autopilot/web/routes/dashboard.py with GET /dashboard route and _get_db helper. Create dashboard.html template extending base.html with 9 stage cards in a responsive Tailwind grid (lg:grid-cols-3, md:grid-cols-2, grid-cols-1). Create partials/stage_card.html with stage name, status placeholder, and id='stage-{name}' for HTMX targeting. Add 'Pipeline not running' conditional banner. Register dashboard_router in routes/__init__.py and include in app.py.; Write failing tests for stage card content with seeded data: (1) ingest card shows green/done status (all 5 jobs done); (2) analyze card shows blue/running status (has running jobs); (3) classify card shows red/error status (has error job); (4) cards show progress text like '5/5' for ingest, '0/3' for analyze; (5) gate badges show correct mode ('auto' for ingest, 'manual' for analyze).; Implement dashboard route logic: query get_current_run(), count_jobs_by_status() for each stage filtered by run_id, get_all_gates(). Compute per-stage: aggregate status (idle/running/waiting/done/error), done/total counts, gate mode. Add status-to-color mapping (grey=idle, blue=running, amber=waiting, green=done, red=error) using Tailwind classes in stage_card.html. Render progress bar and gate badge in each card.; Write failing tests for pipeline timeline bar: (1) dashboard HTML contains overall progress percentage element; (2) contains budget consumed vs remaining display (wall_clock_seconds and budget_remaining_seconds from run); (3) contains run duration display; (4) timeline bar not rendered when no active run.; Create partials/timeline_bar.html with: overall progress bar (total done jobs / total jobs across all stages), budget display (wall_clock_seconds formatted as elapsed, budget_remaining_seconds formatted as remaining), run start time, estimated completion. Wire into dashboard.html below stage cards grid with id='timeline-bar'.; Write failing tests for JSON API endpoints: (1) GET /api/run returns 200 with JSON containing 'run' key; (2) when active run exists, 'run' has run_id, status, current_stage, wall_clock_seconds fields; (3) when no active run, 'run' is null; (4) GET /api/stages returns 200 with JSON list of 9 stage objects; (5) each stage object has 'name', 'status_counts' (dict with pending/running/done/error counts), 'gate_mode' fields.; Implement GET /api/run and GET /api/stages JSON endpoints in dashboard.py. /api/run queries get_current_run() and returns {'run': run_dict_or_null}. /api/stages iterates _PIPELINE_STAGES, calls count_jobs_by_status(stage, run_id) and get_gate(stage), returns list of stage summary dicts.
